primary_link.title: The Dual Mechanisms of Spatial Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

papers.summary: Many multimodal tasks, such as image captioning and visual question answering, require vision-language models (VLMs) to associate objects with their properties and spatial relations. papers.summary_zh: 許多多模態任務，例如 image captioning 和 visual question answering，要求 vision-language models (VLMs) 將物件與其屬性 (properties) 和空間關係 (spatial relations) 聯繫起來。然而，目前尚不清楚這些關聯在 VLMs 內部是如何以及在哪裡計算的。在這項工作中，我們展示了 VLMs 依賴兩種並行機制來表示此類關聯。在 language model backbone 中，中間層在對應於 visual tokens 的基礎上表示與內容無關的 spatial relations。

papers.summary: Multi-agent applications often execute complex tasks as multi-stage workflows, where each stage is an LLM call whose output becomes part of context for subsequent steps. Existing LLM serving systems largely assume homogeneous clusters with identical model replicas. This design overlooks the potential of heterogeneous deployments, where models of different sizes and capabilities enable finer trade-offs between latency and performance. primary_link.title: ACPO: Counteracting Likelihood Displacement in Vision-Language Alignment with Asymmetric Constraints

papers.summary: While Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) has become the de facto approach for aligning Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), it suffers from Likelihood Displacement, where the probability of both chosen and rejected responses collapses. papers.summary_zh: 儘管 Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) 已成為對齊 Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) 的事實方法，但它存在 Likelihood Displacement 的問題，即所選和被拒絕回應的機率都會崩潰。這種優化缺陷在多模態環境中尤其有害：所選似然的侵蝕——我們稱之為 Visual Anchor Collapse 的失敗——導致模型放棄視覺證據，轉而依賴強大的語言先驗，從而引發嚴重的 Hallucination。

papers.summary: Metacognition -- the ability to assess one's own cognitive performance -- is documented across species, with internal confidence estimates serving as a key signal for adaptive behavior. primary_link.title: P^2O: Joint Policy and Prompt Optimization

papers.summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). links.title: VideoDetective: Clue Hunting via both Extrinsic Query and Intrinsic Relevance for Long Video Understanding

primary_link.title: VideoDetective: Clue Hunting via both Extrinsic Query and Intrinsic Relevance for Long Video Understanding

papers.summary: Long video understanding remains challenging for multimodal large language models (MLLMs) due to limited context windows, which necessitate identifying sparse query-relevant video segments. However, existing methods predominantly localize clues based solely on the query, overlooking the video's intrinsic structure and varying relevance across segments. To address this, we propose VideoDetective, a framework that integrates query-to-segment relevance and inter-segment affinity for effective clue hunting in long-video question answering. Specifically, we divide a video into various segments and represent them as a visual-temporal affinity graph built from visual similarity and temporal proximity. We then perform a Hypothesis-Verification-Refinement loop to estimate relevance scores of observed segments to the query and propagate them to unseen segments, yielding a global relevance distribution that guides the localization of the most critical segments for final answering with sparse observation. Experiments show our method consistently achieves substantial gains across a wide range of mainstream MLLMs on representative benchmarks, with accuracy improvements of up to 7.5% on VideoMME-long. papers.summary: Deductive verification provides strong correctness guarantees for code by extracting verification conditions (VCs) and writing formal proofs for them. links.title: AnimalCLAP: Taxonomy-Aware Language-Audio Pretraining for Species Recognition and Trait Inference

primary_link.title: AnimalCLAP: Taxonomy-Aware Language-Audio Pretraining for Species Recognition and Trait Inference

papers.summary: Animal vocalizations provide crucial insights for wildlife assessment, particularly in complex environments such as forests, aiding species identification and ecological monitoring. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled automatic species classification from their vocalizations. However, classifying species unseen during training remains challenging. To address this limitation, we introduce AnimalCLAP, a taxonomy-aware language-audio framework comprising a new dataset and model that incorporate hierarchical biological information. Specifically, our vocalization dataset consists of 4,225 hours of recordings covering 6,823 species, annotated with 22 ecological traits. The AnimalCLAP model is trained on this dataset to align audio and textual representations using taxonomic structures, improving the recognition of unseen species. We demonstrate that our proposed model effectively infers ecological and biological attributes of species directly from their vocalizations, achieving superior performance compared to CLAP. Our dataset, code, and models will be publicly available at https://dahlian00.github.io/AnimalCLAP_Page/.

radar.title_zh: 受人類啟發的巴夫洛夫式和工具式學習用於自主代理導航

radar.summary_zh: 近期透過 reinforcement learning (RL) 實現的 text-to-image (T2I) 生成的進展，受益於評估語義對齊和視覺品質的 reward models。然而，大多數現有的 reward models 對於細粒度的空間關係關注有限，通常生成的圖像整體看起來合理，但在物體定位上存在不準確性。在這項工作中，我們提出了 \textbf{SpatialReward}，這是一個專門設計用於評估生成圖像中空間佈局的可驗證 reward model。

radar.summary_zh: 儘管 large language models (LLMs) 經過大量數據的預訓練，但在專業化、數據稀缺的領域中，它們的知識覆蓋範圍仍然不完整，這促使人們投入大量精力研究用於知識注入的 synthetic data generation。我們提出了 SPA (Scaling Prompt-engineered Augmentation)，這是一個簡單卻難以超越的基準，它使用一小組精心設計的 prompts 來生成用於知識注入的 large-scale synthetic data。透過系統性的比較，我們發現

links.title: Revisiting Quantum Code Generation: Where Should Domain Knowledge Live?

radar.summary_zh: 儘管 large-scale pre-trained image representation models (即 vision encoders) 在各種 vision tasks 中取得了顯著成功，但它們主要在 2D 圖像數據上進行訓練，因此經常無法捕捉現實世界中物體與背景之間的 3D 空間關係，這限制了它們在許多 downstream applications 中的有效性。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 SpatialBoost，這是一個可擴展的框架，它增強了現有 pre-trained vision encod
radar.title: SpatialBoost: Enhancing Visual Representation through Language-Guided Reasoning

Existing approaches predominantly rely on dense embedding models, while learned sparse retrieval (LSR) remains largely unexplored for code. radar.summary_zh: 在大型 codebases 上進行 retrieval 是現代基於 LLM 的 software engineering systems 的關鍵組成部分。現有方法主要依賴於 dense embedding models，而 learned sparse retrieval (LSR) 在程式碼領域仍未被充分探索。然而，將 sparse retrieval 應用於程式碼具有挑戰性，原因包括 subword fragmentation、natural-language queries 和程式碼之間的 semantic gaps、programming languages 和 sub-tasks 的多樣性，以及 code documents 的長度，這些因素可能損害 sparsity 和 lat

links.title: TREX: Trajectory Explanations for Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: TREX: Trajectory Explanations for Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning

radar.summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has demonstrated its ability to solve complex decision-making problems in a variety of domains, by optimizing reward signals obtained through interaction with an environment. However, many real-world scenarios involve multiple, potentially conflicting objectives that cannot be easily represented by a single scalar reward. Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (MORL) addresses this limitation by enabling agents to optimize several objectives simultaneously, explicitly
radar.summary_zh: 強化學習 (RL) 透過最佳化與環境互動獲得的獎勵訊號，展現了其解決各種領域中複雜決策問題的能力。然而，許多真實世界情境涉及多個、可能相互衝突的目標，這些目標無法輕易地由單一純量獎勵來表示。多目標強化學習 (MORL) 透過使代理人能夠同時明確地最佳化多個目標，解決了這一限制。
radar.title: TREX: Trajectory Explanations for Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
radar.title_zh: TREX：多目標強化學習的軌跡解釋

primary_link.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning and The Tale of Two Temporal Difference Errors

radar.summary: The temporal difference (TD) error was first formalized in Sutton (1988), where it was first characterized as the difference between temporally successive predictions, and later, in that same work, formulated as the difference between a bootstrapped target and a prediction. radar.summary_zh: 時間差 (TD) 錯誤最初由 Sutton (1988) 正式化，其中它首先被描述為時間上連續預測之間的差異，隨後在同一著作中，被表述為自舉目標 (bootstrapped target) 與預測之間的差異。從那時起，TD 錯誤的這兩種解釋在文獻中被互換使用，後者最終被深度強化學習 (RL) 架構採用為標準的 critic 損失。I
radar.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning and The Tale of Two Temporal Difference Errors
radar.title_zh: 深度強化學習與兩種時間差錯誤的故事

primary_link.title: Multiperspectivity as a Resource for Narrative Similarity Prediction

radar.summary: Predicting narrative similarity can be understood as an inherently interpretive task: different, equally valid readings of the same text can produce divergent interpretations and thus different similarity judgments, posing a fundamental challenge for semantic evaluation benchmarks that encode a single ground truth. Rather than treating this multiperspectivity as a challenge to overcome, we propose to incorporate it in the decision making process of predictive systems. To explore this strategy, w
radar.summary_zh: 預測敘事相似度可被理解為一項本質上是解釋性的任務：同一文本的不同但同樣有效的解讀會產生分歧的解釋，進而導致不同的相似度判斷，這對編碼單一真實值 (ground truth) 的語義評估基準提出了根本性挑戰。我們建議將這種多視角性納入預測系統的決策過程中，而非將其視為需要克服的挑戰。為了探索這一策略，w
radar.title: Multiperspectivity as a Resource for Narrative Similarity Prediction
radar.title_zh: 多視角性作為敘事相似度預測的資源

links.title: Tuning Real-World Image Restoration at Inference: A Test-Time Scaling Paradigm for Flow Matching Models

primary_link.title: Tuning Real-World Image Restoration at Inference: A Test-Time Scaling Paradigm for Flow Matching Models

radar.summary: Although diffusion-based real-world image restoration (Real-IR) has achieved remarkable progress, efficiently leveraging ultra-large-scale pre-trained text-to-image (T2I) models and fully exploiting their potential remain significant challenges. radar.summary_zh: 儘管基於 diffusion 的真實世界圖像復原 (Real-IR) 已取得了顯著進展，但有效利用超大規模預訓練的 text-to-image (T2I) 模型並充分發揮其潛力仍然是重大挑戰。為了解決這個問題，我們提出 ResFlow-Tuner，這是一個基於最先進的 flow matching 模型 FLUX.1-dev 的圖像復原框架，它將統一多模態融合 (UMMF) 與測試時縮放 (TTS) 相結合，以實現前所未有的復原。
radar.title: Tuning Real-World Image Restoration at Inference: A Test-Time Scaling Paradigm for Flow Matching Models
radar.title_zh: 在推論時調整真實世界圖像復原：Flow Matching 模型的測試時縮放範式

links.title: SecureBreak -- A dataset towards safe and secure models

primary_link.title: SecureBreak -- A dataset towards safe and secure models

radar.summary: Large language models are becoming pervasive core components in many real-world applications. As a consequence, security alignment represents a critical requirement for their safe deployment. Although previous related works focused primarily on model architectures and alignment methodologies, these approaches alone cannot ensure the complete elimination of harmful generations. This concern is reinforced by the growing body of scientific literature showing that attacks, such as jailbreaking and p
radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型正在成為許多真實世界應用中普遍存在的核心組件。因此，安全對齊 (security alignment) 是其安全部署的關鍵要求。儘管先前相關工作主要關注模型架構和對齊方法，但這些方法本身無法確保完全消除有害生成內容。越來越多的科學文獻表明，諸如 jailbreaking 和 p 等攻擊，加劇了這一擔憂。

radar.title_zh: SecureBreak — 一個旨在建立安全模型的數據集

links.title: The Golden Subspace: Where Efficiency Meets Generalization in Continual Test-Time Adaptation

primary_link.title: The Golden Subspace: Where Efficiency Meets Generalization in Continual Test-Time Adaptation

radar.summary: Continual Test-Time Adaptation (CTTA) aims to enable models to adapt online to unlabeled data streams under distribution shift without accessing source data. Existing CTTA methods face an efficiency-generalization trade-off: updating more parameters improves adaptation but severely reduces online inference efficiency. An ideal solution is to achieve comparable adaptation with minimal feature updates; we call this minimal subspace the golden subspace. We prove its existence in a single-step adapt
radar.summary_zh: 持續測試時適應 (Continual Test-Time Adaptation, CTTA) 旨在使模型能夠在不存取來源資料的情況下，在分佈偏移下線上適應未標記的資料流。現有的 CTTA 方法面臨著效率與泛化能力的權衡：更新更多參數可改善適應性，但會嚴重降低線上推斷 (inference) 效率。一種理想的解決方案是透過最少的特徵更新來實現可比較的適應性；我們將這個最小子空間稱為「黃金子空間」(golden subspace)。我們證明了它在單步適應中的存在。
radar.title: The Golden Subspace: Where Efficiency Meets Generalization in Continual Test-Time Adaptation
radar.title_zh: 黄金子空間：持續測試時適應中效率與泛化能力的交匯點

links.title: SHARP: Spectrum-aware Highly-dynamic Adaptation for Resolution Promotion in Remote Sensing Synthesis

primary_link.title: SHARP: Spectrum-aware Highly-dynamic Adaptation for Resolution Promotion in Remote Sensing Synthesis

radar.summary: Text-to-image generation powered by Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) has made remarkable strides, yet remote sensing (RS) synthesis lags behind due to two barriers: the absence of a domain-specialized DiT prior and the prohibitive cost of training at the large resolutions that RS applications demand. Training-free resolution promotion via Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) rescaling offers a practical remedy, but every existing method applies a static positional scaling rule throughout the denoising
radar.summary_zh: 經由 Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) 驅動的文字到圖像生成 (Text-to-image generation) 已取得顯著進展，然而遙感 (RS) 合成仍滯後，原因有二：缺乏領域專用的 DiT 先驗知識，以及在 RS 應用所需的較大解析度下訓練的成本過高。透過 Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) 重新縮放 (rescaling) 實現的免訓練解析度提升提供了一種實用的補救措施，但所有現有方法在整個去噪 (denoising) 過程中都應用了靜態位置縮放規則。
radar.title: SHARP: Spectrum-aware Highly-dynamic Adaptation for Resolution Promotion in Remote Sensing Synthesis
radar.title_zh: SHARP：用於遙感合成中解析度提升的頻譜感知高動態適應

links.title: SLURP-TN : Resource for Tunisian Dialect Spoken Language Understanding

primary_link.title: SLURP-TN : Resource for Tunisian Dialect Spoken Language Understanding

radar.summary: Spoken Language Understanding (SLU) aims to extract the semantic information from the speech utterance of user queries. It is a core component in a task-oriented dialogue system. With the spectacular progress of deep neural network models and the evolution of pre-trained language models, SLU has obtained significant breakthroughs. However, only a few high-resource languages have taken advantage of this progress due to the absence of SLU resources. In this paper, we seek to mitigate this obstacle
radar.summary_zh: 語音理解 (Spoken Language Understanding, SLU) 旨在從用戶查詢的語音語句中提取語義資訊。它是任務導向對話系統 (task-oriented dialogue system) 中的一個核心組件。隨著深度神經網路模型 (deep neural network models) 的顯著進步以及預訓練語言模型 (pre-trained language models) 的發展，SLU 已取得了重大突破。然而，由於 SLU 資源的匱乏，只有少數高資源語言受益於這項進展。在本文中，我們試圖緩解這一障礙。
radar.title: SLURP-TN : Resource for Tunisian Dialect Spoken Language Understanding
radar.title_zh: SLURP-TN：突尼斯方言語音理解資源

links.title: IGV-RRT: Prior-Real-Time Observation Fusion for Active Object Search in Changing Environments

primary_link.title: IGV-RRT: Prior-Real-Time Observation Fusion for Active Object Search in Changing Environments

radar.summary: Object Goal Navigation (ObjectNav) in temporally changing indoor environments is challenging because object relocation can invalidate historical scene knowledge. To address this issue, we propose a probabilistic planning framework that combines uncertainty-aware scene priors with online target relevance estimates derived from a Vision Language Model (VLM). The framework contains a dual-layer semantic mapping module and a real-time planner. The mapping module includes an Information Gain Map (IGM
radar.summary_zh: 在時間動態變化的室內環境中進行物件目標導航 (Object Goal Navigation, ObjectNav) 具有挑戰性，因為物件的重新定位可能使歷史場景知識失效。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個機率規劃框架，該框架將不確定性感知的場景先驗知識與從 Vision Language Model (VLM) 導出的線上目標相關性估計相結合。該框架包含一個雙層語義映射模組 (semantic mapping module) 和一個即時規劃器 (real-time planner)。該映射模組包括一個資訊增益圖 (Information Gain Map, IGM)。
radar.title: IGV-RRT: Prior-Real-Time Observation Fusion for Active Object Search in Changing Environments
radar.title_zh: IGV-RRT：用於變化環境中主動物件搜尋的先驗與即時觀測融合

links.title: When Exploration Comes for Free with Mixture-Greedy: Do we need UCB in Diversity-Aware Multi-Armed Bandits?

primary_link.title: When Exploration Comes for Free with Mixture-Greedy: Do we need UCB in Diversity-Aware Multi-Armed Bandits?

radar.summary: Efficient selection among multiple generative models is increasingly important in modern generative AI, where sampling from suboptimal models is costly. This problem can be formulated as a multi-armed bandit task. Under diversity-aware evaluation metrics, a non-degenerate mixture of generators can outperform any individual model, distinguishing this setting from classical best-arm identification. Prior approaches therefore incorporate an Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) exploration bonus into the mi
radar.summary_zh: 在現代生成式 AI (generative AI) 中，多個生成模型之間的有效選擇變得越來越重要，因為從次優模型中取樣成本高昂。這個問題可以被表述為一個多臂老虎機 (multi-armed bandit) 任務。在考量多樣性的評估指標下，非退化 (non-degenerate) 的生成器組合可以超越任何單一模型，這使得這種設定有別於經典的最佳臂識別 (best-arm identification)。因此，先前的方法將 Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) 探索獎勵納入到混合中。
radar.title: When Exploration Comes for Free with Mixture-Greedy: Do we need UCB in Diversity-Aware Multi-Armed Bandits?
radar.title_zh: 當 Mixture-Greedy 提供免費探索時：在考量多樣性的多臂老虎機 (Multi-Armed Bandits) 中我們還需要 UCB 嗎？

links.title: Scaling DoRA: High-Rank Adaptation via Factored Norms and Fused Kernels

primary_link.title: Scaling DoRA: High-Rank Adaptation via Factored Norms and Fused Kernels

top5.summary:
Weight-Decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation (DoRA) extends LoRA by decoupling weight magnitude from direction, but its forward pass requires the row-wise norm of W + sBA, a computation that every major framework we surveyed implements by materializing the dense [d_out, d_in] product BA. At d_in = 8192 and rank r = 384, a single module's norm requires about 512 MB of transient working memory in bf16, making high-rank DoRA costly and often infeasible on common single-GPU setups once hundreds of adapted modules and checkpointing are involved.
  We present two systems contributions. A factored norm decomposes the squared norm into base, cross, and Gram terms computable through O(d_out r + r^2) intermediates, eliminating the dense product. Fused Triton kernels collapse the four-kernel DoRA composition into a single pass, reducing memory traffic by about 4x and using a numerically stable form that avoids catastrophic cancellation in the near-unity rescaling regime where magnitude scales concentrate in practice.
  Across six 8-32B vision-language models (VLMs) on three NVIDIA GPUs (RTX 6000 PRO, H200, B200) at r = 384 in bf16, the fused implementation is 1.5-2.0x faster than Hugging Face PEFT's DoRA implementation for inference and 1.5-1.9x faster for gradient computation (optimizer step excluded), with up to 7 GB lower peak VRAM. Microbenchmarks on six GPUs spanning four architecture generations (L40S, A100, RTX 6000 PRO, H200, B200, B300) confirm 1.5-2.7x compose-kernel speedup. Final-logit cosine similarity exceeds 0.9999 across all model/GPU pairs, and multi-seed training curves match within 7.1 x 10^-4 mean per-step loss delta over 2000 steps.
top5.summary_zh:
Weight-Decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation (DoRA) 透過將權重大小與方向解耦來擴展 LoRA，但其前向傳播需要 W + sBA 的行向範數（row-wise norm），而我們調查的每個主要框架都透過具體化密集型 [d_out, d_in] 乘積 BA 來實現此運算。在 d_in = 8192 和秩 r = 384 的情況下，單個模組的範數在 bf16 中需要約 512 MB 的瞬態工作記憶體，這使得高秩 DoRA 在數百個已適應模組和檢查點參與時變得昂貴，且在常見的單 GPU 設定上往往不可行。
我們提出了兩項系統貢獻。分解範數（factored norm）將平方範數分解為可透過 O(d_out r + r^2) 中間值計算的基底項、交叉項和 Gram 項，從而消除了密集型乘積。融合 Triton kernels 將四個 kernel 的 DoRA 組合縮減為單次傳遞，將記憶體流量減少約 4 倍，並使用數值穩定形式，避免了在實際中大小尺度集中在接近統一重縮放（near-unity rescaling）範圍時的災難性抵消（catastrophic cancellation）。
在六個 8-32B 的 vision-language models (VLMs) 上，於三個 NVIDIA GPUs (RTX 6000 PRO, H200, B200) 以 r = 384 運行 bf16 時，融合實現的推理速度比 Hugging Face PEFT 的 DoRA 實現快 1.5-2.0 倍，梯度計算速度快 1.5-1.9 倍（不包括優化器步驟），且峰值 VRAM 最多降低 7 GB。在涵蓋四代架構的六個 GPUs (L40S, A100, RTX 6000 PRO, H200, B200, B300) 上的微基準測試證實，組合核心（compose-kernel）速度提升 1.5-2.7 倍。所有模型/GPU 對的最終 logit 餘弦相似度超過 0.9999，且在 2000 個步驟中，多種子訓練曲線的平均每步損失差值在 7.1 x 10^-4 以內。
top5.title: Scaling DoRA: High-Rank Adaptation via Factored Norms and Fused Kernels
top5.title_zh: Scaling DoRA: 透過分解範數和融合核心實現高秩適應

links.title: Speed by Simplicity: A Single-Stream Architecture for Fast Audio-Video Generative Foundation Model

primary_link.title: Speed by Simplicity: A Single-Stream Architecture for Fast Audio-Video Generative Foundation Model

top5.summary: We present daVinci-MagiHuman, an open-source audio-video generative foundation model for human-centric generation. daVinci-MagiHuman jointly generates synchronized video and audio using a single-stream Transformer that processes text, video, and audio within a unified token sequence via self-attention only. This single-stream design avoids the complexity of multi-stream or cross-attention architectures while remaining easy to optimize with standard training and inference infrastructure. The model is particularly strong in human-centric scenarios, producing expressive facial performance, natural speech-expression coordination, realistic body motion, and precise audio-video synchronization. It supports multilingual spoken generation across Chinese (Mandarin and Cantonese), English, Japanese, Korean, German, and French. For efficient inference, we combine the single-stream backbone with model distillation, latent-space super-resolution, and a Turbo VAE decoder, enabling generation of a 5-second 256p video in 2 seconds on a single H100 GPU. In automatic evaluation, daVinci-MagiHuman achieves the highest visual quality and text alignment among leading open models, along with the lowest word error rate (14.60%) for speech intelligibility. In pairwise human evaluation, it achieves win rates of 80.0% against Ovi 1.1 and 60.9% against LTX 2.3 over 2000 comparisons. We open-source the complete model stack, including the base model, the distilled model, the super-resolution model, and the inference codebase.
top5.summary_zh: 我們推出了 daVinci-MagiHuman，一個開源的音訊-視訊生成基礎模型，專為以人為中心的生成任務設計。daVinci-MagiHuman 使用單流 Transformer，透過純粹的 self-attention，將文字、視訊和音訊在統一的 token 序列中進行處理，從而共同生成同步的視訊和音訊。這種單流設計避免了多流或 cross-attention 架構的複雜性，同時仍能透過標準的訓練和推理基礎設施輕鬆優化。該模型在以人為中心的場景中表現尤其出色，能產生富有表現力的面部表演、自然的語音表達協調、逼真的身體動作以及精確的音訊-視訊同步。它支援多語言語音生成，涵蓋中文（普通話和粵語）、英語、日語、韓語、德語和法語。為了實現高效推理，我們將單流骨幹網路與 model distillation、latent-space super-resolution 和 Turbo VAE decoder 相結合，使得在單個 H100 GPU 上僅需 2 秒即可生成 5 秒的 256p 視訊。在自動評估中，daVinci-MagiHuman 在領先的開源模型中取得了最高的視覺品質和文字對齊度，同時語音清晰度的詞錯誤率（word error rate）最低（14.60%）。在成對的人工評估中，它在 2000 次比較中，對 Ovi 1.1 取得了 80.0% 的勝率，對 LTX 2.3 取得了 60.9% 的勝率。我們開源了完整的模型堆疊，包括基礎模型、distilled model、super-resolution model 和推理程式碼庫。
top5.title: Speed by Simplicity: A Single-Stream Architecture for Fast Audio-Video Generative Foundation Model
top5.title_zh: 以簡馭繁：用於快速音訊-視訊生成基礎模型的單流架構

primary_link.title: Thinking Deeper, Not Longer: Depth-Recurrent Transformers for Compositional Generalization

top5.summary_zh: 標準的 Transformer 具有固定的計算深度，從根本上限制了它們泛化到需要可變深度推理的任務的能力，例如多跳圖遍歷或巢狀邏輯。我們提出了一種深度遞歸 Transformer，它透過在 latent space 中迭代應用共享權重的 Transformer 區塊，將計算深度從參數數量中解耦——這使得模型在推理時能夠以遞歸步驟換取更深的推理能力。我們的架構包含了三個機制。

links.title: MemDLM: Memory-Enhanced DLM Training

primary_link.title: MemDLM: Memory-Enhanced DLM Training

top5.summary: Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) offer attractive advantages over Auto-Regressive (AR) models, such as full-attention parallel decoding and flexible generation. However, they suffer from a notable train-inference mismatch: DLMs are trained with a static, single-step masked prediction objective, but deployed through a multi-step progressive denoising trajectory. We propose MemDLM (Memory-Enhanced DLM), which narrows this gap by embedding a simulated denoising process into training via Bi-level Optimization. An inner loop updates a set of fast weights, forming a Parametric Memory that captures the local trajectory experience of each sample, while an outer loop updates the base model conditioned on this memory. By offloading memorization pressure from token representations to parameters, MemDLM yields faster convergence and lower training loss. Moreover, the inner loop can be re-enabled at inference time as an adaptation step, yielding additional gains on long-context understanding. We find that, when activated at inference time, this Parametric Memory acts as an emergent in-weight retrieval mechanism, helping MemDLM further reduce token-level attention bottlenecks on challenging Needle-in-a-Haystack retrieval tasks. Code: https://github.com/JarvisPei/MemDLM.
top5.summary_zh: Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) 相較於 Auto-Regressive (AR) 模型，提供了諸多吸引人的優勢，例如 full-attention 並行解碼和靈活的生成。然而，它們存在著顯著的訓練-推理不匹配問題：DLMs 以靜態的、單步的 masked prediction 目標進行訓練，但透過多步漸進式去噪軌跡進行部署。我們提出了 MemDLM (Memory-Enhanced DLM)，它透過 Bi-level Optimization 將模擬的去噪過程嵌入訓練中，從而縮小了這一差距。內部循環更新一組 fast weights，形成一個 Parametric Memory，捕獲每個樣本的局部軌跡經驗，而外部循環則根據這個記憶體更新基礎模型。透過將記憶負擔從 token 表徵卸載到參數，MemDLM 實現了更快的收斂和更低的訓練損失。此外，內部循環可以在推理時重新啟用作為一個適應步驟，從而在長上下文理解方面獲得額外收益。我們發現，當在推理時激活時，這個 Parametric Memory 充當一種 emergent in-weight retrieval 機制，幫助 MemDLM 進一步減少在具有挑戰性的 Needle-in-a-Haystack 檢索任務上的 token 級別 attention 瓶頸。程式碼：https://github.com/JarvisPei/MemDLM。
top5.title: MemDLM: Memory-Enhanced DLM Training
top5.title_zh: MemDLM: 記憶體增強型 DLM 訓練

links.title: On the Direction of RLVR Updates for LLM Reasoning: Identification and Exploitation

primary_link.title: On the Direction of RLVR Updates for LLM Reasoning: Identification and Exploitation

top5.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has substantially improved the reasoning capabilities of large language models. While existing analyses identify that RLVR-induced changes are sparse, they primarily focus on the magnitude of these updates, largely overlooking their direction. In this work, we argue that the direction of updates is a more critical lens for understanding RLVR's effects, which can be captured by the signed, token-level log probability difference Δlog p between the base and final RLVR models. Through statistical analysis and token-replacement interventions, we demonstrate that Δlog p more effectively identifies sparse, yet reasoning-critical updates than magnitude-based metrics (\eg divergence or entropy). Building on this insight, we propose two practical applications: (1) a test-time extrapolation method that amplifies the policy along the learned Δlog p direction to improve reasoning accuracy without further training; (2) a training-time reweighting method that focuses learning on low-probability (corresponding to higher Δlog p) tokens, which improves reasoning performance across models and benchmarks. Our work establishes the direction of change as a key principle for analyzing and improving RLVR.
top5.summary_zh: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) 大幅提升了大型語言模型（LLM）的推理能力。雖然現有分析指出 RLVR 引起的變化是稀疏的，但它們主要關注這些更新的幅度，而很大程度上忽略了其方向。在這項工作中，我們認為更新的方向是理解 RLVR 影響更為關鍵的視角，這可以透過基礎模型和最終 RLVR 模型之間有符號的、token 級別的對數機率差 Δlog p 來捕捉。透過統計分析和 token 替換干預，我們證明 Δlog p 比基於幅度的指標（例如 divergence 或 entropy）更有效地識別稀疏但對推理至關重要的更新。基於此洞察，我們提出了兩個實用應用：(1) 一種測試時外推（test-time extrapolation）方法，它沿著學到的 Δlog p 方向放大策略，以在不進行進一步訓練的情況下提高推理準確性；(2) 一種訓練時重加權（training-time reweighting）方法，它將學習重點放在低機率（對應更高的 Δlog p）的 tokens 上，這提高了跨模型和基準測試的推理性能。我們的工作將變化方向確立為分析和改進 RLVR 的關鍵原則。
top5.title: On the Direction of RLVR Updates for LLM Reasoning: Identification and Exploitation
top5.title_zh: 關於 LLM 推理的 RLVR 更新方向：識別與利用
